headline: Which AI Server Stock Has Dominated in 2026: Super Micro, Dell, or Hewlett Packard Enterprise?
summary: There’s a clear, undeniable winner this year among three major AI server stocks. Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) stock has dominated 2026, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) a strong second and Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) a distant third. The gap between the three is striking, given that all three are riding the same AI infrastructure wave. As ... headline: TensorX Launches With €8M Seed Funding Round Led by Darius Cubed Ventures for Bet on European Sovereign AI Infrastructure With Plans to Deploy up to €100M in NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs
summary: DUBLIN, June 24, 2026--A team of Irish founders has committed €8 million to Nvidia Blackwell GPUs, including the latest B300 chips, to launch TensorX, a sovereign AI inference platform designed for Europe's AI builders, trusted by regulated industries and already generating revenue from paying customers. The company was founded by Shane Morton, is part of the NVIDIA Inception program and is partnering with Dell on sourcing GPU hardware.

headline: Dell (DELL) Launches PowerEdge XE8812 To Push Deeper Into Rack Scale AI
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) has introduced the PowerEdge XE8812 server featuring NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL4 architecture. The system is designed for high performance AI, scientific and engineering workloads with direct liquid cooling. This launch positions Dell within the emerging shift from standalone GPU servers to integrated, rack scale AI infrastructure. The company is supplying systems to large AI and supercomputing customers, including CoreWeave and Lawrence Berkeley National...
